title: cyathea macgillivrayi
intro: Cyathea macgillivrayi, also known as the Black Tree Fern, is a species of tree fern native to New Zealand. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that have been traditionally used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Cyathea macgillivrayi include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory diseases such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and also used in topical applications such as ointments and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
